top_categories.workout_context: 8,415ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Inthanon Challenge's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before February.
Inthanon Challenge's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 30 miles.
30 miles on mixed terrain burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
Base fitness for Inthanon Challenge. Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.
280ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on mixed terrain. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 8,415ft of climbing on mixed terrain does to your power file on race day.
At Inthanon Challenge, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Inthanon Challenge. Train it until it feels boring.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Inthanon Challenge, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
Below LT1, you burn fat. Above it, you burn glycogen. For 30 miles, a higher LT1 means slower bonk.

race_overlay.nutrition: Standard hydration and fueling. One bottle per hour minimum. Gel or bar every 45 minutes at race intensity.
race_overlay.altitude: Inthanon Challenge with 8,415ft of climbing, much of it above 8,000ft demands altitude preparation. Arrive 5–7 days early for acclimatization. Expect 5–15% power reduction at altitude. Increase iron intake 4 weeks out. Hydrate aggressively — altitude increases fluid loss by 20–40%.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on VO2max, Race Simulation, and Gravel Specific to prepare you for 30 miles of mixed terrain in Chom Thong, Chiang Mai, Thailand.
